name: Team Kasich
subject: The latest
body:
Gov. John Kasich issued the following statement today following
the Emmanuel Macron victory in the French election.

Peter, Gov. John Kasich issued
the following statement today following the Emmanuel Macron
victory in the French election:

Congratulations to
President-elect Emmanuel Macron on his critical victory. France
is our oldest ally and her commitment to trade & NATO sends a
strong message to the world. #FrenchElection2017

The governor was also a guest on
CNN's State of the Union - watch the interview here:

Governor Kasich's new book Two
Path: America Divided or United made the New York Time's
Bestseller's list. Two Paths is the Governor's 4th New York Times
bestseller.

Gov. Kasich concluded the launch
of his book with an event at the Nixon Presidential Library
(watch event here). Throughout the book tour, Governor Kasich was
greeted by enthusiastic crowds that responded to his message of
uniting our country to solve our greatest challenges.

Gov. Kasich also took a few
moments to view the letter he wrote to President Nixon. The
result, an invitation by the President to visit him at the White
House. This was an important event in Gov. Kasich's decision to
enter a career in public service.

Gov. Kasich with President Nixon
in the Oval Office:

In case you missed the news about
our exclusive offer, we have some copies of Gov. Kasich's new
book, Two Paths: America Divided or United, and we'd like to send
you a copy as a thank you if you'll make a generous contribution
to help us spread his message.

>>Chip in $50 Now<<
Receive a copy of the book

>>Chip in $150 or More<<
Receive a signed copy of the book

The dollars we raise go directly
into helping tell not only the story about the work he's doing to
strengthen Ohio, but also provide a hopeful and more inclusive
path forward for our country.

In case you missed some of the
governor's recent news interviews, you can watch them below, as
well as a recap video from one of the Ohio book signing events:

Discussion with David Axelrod at
the University of Chicago

The Rich Eisen Show

Real Time with Bill Maher

Fox News w/ Martha MacCallum

Paid for by Kasich
for America, not authorized by any candidate or candidate's
committee.
www.JohnKasich.com

This message was intended for: dpwillis67@gmail.com
You were added to the system July 18, 2015.
For more information please follow the URL below:
http://email.sermodigital.com/p/iWy2orw2gN

Follow the URL below to update your preferences or opt-out:
http://email.sermodigital.com/p/oWy2orw2gN

To unsubscribe from future mailings, send an email to mailto:unsub-43827513885-echo3-B81638196AA5E19A1874E49E6B4C85A9@emailsendr.net?Subject=Unsubscribe&body=Please%20remove%20me%20from%20further%20mailings
with "Unsubscribe" as the subject line.

name: Health Care Update (via DSCC)
subject: Pre-existing conditions under Trumpcare
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: Learning through play
body:
what our kids need
#
Team --
I'm fortunate enough to be on paternity leave while we wait for the imminent arrival of our third child -- something I will make possible for every parent in Florida when I'm Governor. But that's an email for another day.
Today, I want to talk to you about early childhood education, and what our goals are here in Florida. While we've been soaking up as much time as possible with our almost three-year-old twins, Caroline and Jackson, before their brother arrives, I've been thinking a lot about the needs of every young child in the state -- and how we can ensure those needs are met.
Right now, we begin most of our public education at five years old -- but research tells us that 90% of a child's brain development happens from ages zero to five. I firmly believe we need to invest more in those earliest years through research-based programs that help our children learn, grow and thrive.
When we talk about wanting to start education earlier, we aren't talking about sitting two year olds down and getting them to bubble in a multiple choice test. Our goal for earlier childhood education is one that is focused on learning through play -- what research tells us is most effective. We're talking about coloring, learning to share, building blocks, and story time.
**[If you agree that we need this kind of research-based early childhood education in Florida, add your name to our petition now>>](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb337a/9e00690/4818ab18/24034822/359610950/VEsH/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/ece")**
Together, we can make sure that every child in Florida has the opportunity to be the best that they can be.
Bring it home,
Andrew
**[CONTRIBUTE](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb337a/9e00690/4818ab18/24034825/359610950/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ag-may2017?refcode=em20170507-ece")**

Paid by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is one of the most important tools we have to reach supporters like you, but you can let us know if you'd like to [receive fewer emails](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb337a/9e00690/4818ab18/24034824/359610950/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiJ9/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip="). We'd hate to see you go, but if you need to do so, click here to [unsubscribe](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb337a/9e00690/4818ab18/24034827/359610950/VEsC/ "https://gillum.cp.bsd.net/page/unsubscribe/"). [You can support our campaign by making a donation.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb337a/9e00690/4818ab18/24034826/359610950/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ag-footer")

name: Maggie Hassan
subject: No one should have to go through this
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Hello --
As May kicks off, students across the country are getting ready for graduation. This should be a time to celebrate, but for many people, a dark cloud hangs over the occasion.
One in five women who attend college report being sexually assaulted. Even one person is too many, and we need to take action now.
That's why I just joined a bipartisan coalition of my colleagues in the Senate and reintroduced the Campus Accountability and Safety Act.
[**We need our schools to take action, we need solutions, and we need to end the epidemic of sexual assault on college campuses. Will you add your name to join me and tell Congress to pass this bill to protect students from sexual assault?**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdd46/2a380d31/15660e51/fef4e5/3592703500/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "https://petitions.signforgood.com/stopcampussexualassault/?code=hassan&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")
Students should have to worry about getting good grades and developing the skills the need to be successful, not live in fear of sexual assault. No one should feel unsafe at her or his place of learning, and no survivor should be silenced.
With your help, we can hold schools accountable and take the necessary steps to end this all too common heartbreak.
[**Please, add your name to urge Congress to pass legislation to end sexual assault and protect survivors.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdd46/2a380d31/15660e51/fef4e5/3592703500/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "https://petitions.signforgood.com/stopcampussexualassault/?code=hassan&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")
Thank you for speaking up, and thank you for everything you do.
With every good wish,
Senator Maggie Hassan

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
To unsubscribe from the Maggie for NH email list, please click here: [Unsubscribe](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdd46/2a380d31/15660e51/fef4e6/3592703500/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com")

Paid for by Maggie for NH

Contributions or gifts to Maggie for NH are not tax deductible.

Maggie for NH
PO Box 298
Concord, NH 03302 United States
